22	class TopKTopPSampler(nn.Module):
23	    """
24	    Module that performs optional top-k and top-p filtering followed by
25	    weighted random sampling of logits.
26	
27	    Implementations may update the logits tensor in-place.
28	    """
29	
30	    def __init__(self):
31	        super().__init__()
32	        if current_platform.is_cuda():
33	            if is_flashinfer_available:
34	                flashinfer_version = flashinfer.__version__
35	                if flashinfer_version < "0.2.3":
36	                    logger.warning(
37	                        "FlashInfer version >= 0.2.3 required. "
38	                        "Falling back to default sampling implementation.")
39	                    self.forward = self.forward_native
40	                elif envs.VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER is not False:
41	                    # NOTE(woosuk): The V0 sampler doesn't use FlashInfer for
42	                    # sampling unless VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=1 (i.e., by
43	                    # default it is unused). For backward compatibility, we set
44	                    # `VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER` as None by default and
45	                    # interpret it differently in V0 and V1 samplers: In V0,
46	                    # None means False, while in V1, None means True. This is
47	                    # why we use the condition
48	                    # `envs.VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER is not False` here.
49	                    logger.info("Using FlashInfer for top-p & top-k sampling.")
50	                    self.forward = self.forward_cuda
51	                else:
52	                    logger.warning(
53	                        "FlashInfer is available, but it is not enabled. "
54	                        "Falling back to the PyTorch-native implementation of "
55	                        "top-p & top-k sampling. For the best performance, "
56	                        "please set VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=1.")
57	                    self.forward = self.forward_native
58	            else:
59	                logger.warning(
60	                    "FlashInfer is not available. Falling back to the PyTorch-"
61	                    "native implementation of top-p & top-k sampling. For the "
62	                    "best performance, please install FlashInfer.")
63	                self.forward = self.forward_native
64	        elif current_platform.is_tpu():
65	            self.forward = self.forward_tpu
66	        else:
67	            self.forward = self.forward_native
68	
69	    def forward_native(
70	        self,
71	        logits: torch.Tensor,
72	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
73	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
74	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
75	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
76	        """
77	        PyTorch-native implementation of top-k and top-p sampling.
78	
79	        The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
80	        """
81	        logits = apply_top_k_top_p(logits, k, p)
82	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
83	        return random_sample(probs, generators)
84	
85	    def forward_cuda(
86	        self,
87	        logits: torch.Tensor,
88	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
89	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
90	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
91	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
92	        """More optimized implementation for top-k and top-p sampling."""
93	        if k is None and p is None:
94	            # We prefer `random_sample` over `flashinfer_sample` when sorting is
95	            # not needed. This is because `random_sample` does not require
96	            # CPU-GPU synchronization while `flashinfer_sample` does.
97	            probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
98	            return random_sample(probs, generators)
99	        if generators:
100	            logger.warning("FlashInfer 0.2.3+ does not support "
101	                           "per-request generators. Falling back to "
102	                           "PyTorch-native implementation.")
103	            return self.forward_native(logits, generators, k, p)
104	        # flashinfer sampling functions expect contiguous logits.
105	        # In flex_attn/triton_attn fp32 inference, logits can be non-contiguous
106	        # because of slicing operation in logits_processor.
107	        return flashinfer_sample(logits.contiguous(), k, p, generators)
108	
109	    def forward_tpu(
110	        self,
111	        logits: torch.Tensor,
112	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
113	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
114	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
115	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
116	        logits = apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(logits, k, p)
117	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
118	        return random_sample(probs, generators)
119	
120	
121	def apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(
122	    logits: torch.Tensor,
123	    k: torch.Tensor,
124	    p: torch.Tensor,
125	) -> torch.Tensor:
126	    """
127	    Apply top-k and top-p optimized for TPU.
128	
129	    This algorithm avoids using torch.scatter which is extremely slow on TPU.
130	    This is achieved by finding a "cut-off" element in the original logit, and
131	    after thresholding the logit using this cut-off, the remaining elements
132	    shall constitute the top-p set.
133	
134	    Note: in the case of tie (i.e. multipple cut-off elements present in the
135	    logit), all tie elements are included in the top-p set. In other words,
136	    this function does not break ties. Instead, these tie tokens have equal
137	    chance of being chosen during final sampling, so we can consider the tie
138	    being broken then.
139	    """
140	    probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1)
141	    probs_sort, _ = probs.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
142	
143	    if k is not None:
144	        top_k_count = probs_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: (batch, )
145	        top_k_count = top_k_count.unsqueeze(dim=1)
146	        top_k_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_k_count)
147	
148	        # Make sure the no top-k rows are no-op.
149	        no_top_k_mask = (k == logits.shape[1]).unsqueeze(dim=1)
150	        top_k_cutoff.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
151	
152	        elements_to_discard = probs < top_k_cutoff
153	        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
154	
155	    if p is not None:
156	        cumprob = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1)
157	        top_p_mask = cumprob <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
158	        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # at least one
159	
160	        top_p_count = top_p_mask.sum(dim=-1).unsqueeze(1)
161	        top_p_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_p_count)
162	        elements_to_discard = probs < top_p_cutoff
163	        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
164	
165	    return logits
166	
167	
168	def apply_top_k_top_p(
169	    logits: torch.Tensor,
170	    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
171	    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
172	) -> torch.Tensor:
173	    """Apply top-k and top-p masks to the logits.
174	
175	    If a top-p is used, this function will sort the logits tensor,
176	    which can be slow for large batches.
177	
178	    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
179	    """
180	    if p is None:
181	        if k is None:
182	            return logits
183	
184	        # Avoid sorting vocab for top-k only case.
185	        return apply_top_k_only(logits, k)
186	
187	    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
188	
189	    if k is not None:
190	        # Apply top-k.
191	        top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: B
192	        # Get all the top_k values.
193	        top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
194	        top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
195	        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
196	
197	    if p is not None:
198	        # Apply top-p.
199	        probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
200	        probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1, out=probs_sort)
201	        top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
202	        # at least one
203	        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
204	        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
205	
206	    # Re-sort the probabilities.
207	    logits = logits_sort.scatter(dim=-1, index=logits_idx, src=logits_sort)
208	    return logits
209	
210	
211	def apply_top_k_only(
212	    logits: torch.Tensor,
213	    k: torch.Tensor,
214	) -> torch.Tensor:
215	    """
216	    Apply top-k mask to the logits.
217	
218	    This implementation doesn't involve sorting the entire vocab.
219	
220	    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
221	    """
222	    no_top_k_mask = k == logits.shape[1]
223	    # Set non-top-k rows to 1 so that we can gather.
224	    k = k.masked_fill(no_top_k_mask, 1)
225	    max_top_k = k.max()
226	    # topk.values tensor has shape [batch_size, max_top_k].
227	    # Convert top k to 0-based index in range [0, max_top_k).
228	    k_index = k.sub_(1).unsqueeze(1)
229	    top_k_mask = logits.topk(max_top_k, dim=1).values.gather(1, k_index.long())
230	    # Handle non-topk rows.
231	    top_k_mask.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask.unsqueeze(1), -float("inf"))
232	    logits.masked_fill_(logits < top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
233	    return logits
234	
235	
236	def random_sample(
237	    probs: torch.Tensor,
238	    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
239	) -> torch.Tensor:
240	    """Randomly sample from the probabilities.
241	
242	    We use this function instead of torch.multinomial because torch.multinomial
243	    causes CPU-GPU synchronization.
244	    """
245	    q = torch.empty_like(probs)
246	    # NOTE(woosuk): To batch-process the requests without their own seeds,
247	    # which is the common case, we first assume that every request does
248	    # not have its own seed. Then, we overwrite the values for the requests
249	    # that have their own seeds.
250	    if len(generators) != probs.shape[0]:
251	        q.exponential_()
252	    if generators:
253	        # TODO(woosuk): This can be slow because we handle each request
254	        # one by one. Optimize this.
255	        for i, generator in generators.items():
256	            q[i].exponential_(generator=generator)
257	    return probs.div_(q).argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)
258	
259	
260	def flashinfer_sample(
261	    logits: torch.Tensor,
262	    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
263	    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
264	    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
265	) -> torch.Tensor:
266	    """Sample from the logits using FlashInfer.
267	
268	    Statistically, this function is equivalent to the `random_sample` function.
269	    However, this function is faster because it avoids sorting the logits tensor
270	    via rejection sampling.
271	
272	    NOTE: The outputs of this function do not necessarily match the outputs of
273	    the `random_sample` function. It only guarantees that the outputs are
274	    statistically equivalent.
275	
276	    NOTE: This function includes CPU-GPU synchronization, while `random_sample`
277	    does not. Call this function at the end of the forward pass to minimize
278	    the synchronization overhead.
279	    """
280	    assert not (k is None and p is None)
281	    if k is None:
282	        # Top-p only.
283	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
284	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_p_sampling_from_probs(
285	            probs, p, deterministic=True)
286	    elif p is None:
287	        # Top-k only.
288	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
289	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_sampling_from_probs(
290	            probs, k, deterministic=True)
291	    else:
292	        # Both top-k and top-p.
293	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_top_p_sampling_from_logits(
294	            logits, k, p, deterministic=True)
295	
296	    return next_token_ids.view(-1)
297

33	class OpenAIBaseModel(BaseModel):
34	    # OpenAI API does allow extra fields
35	    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="allow")
36	
37	    # Cache class field names
38	    field_names: ClassVar[Optional[set[str]]] = None
39	
40	    @model_validator(mode="wrap")
41	    @classmethod
42	    def __log_extra_fields__(cls, data, handler):
43	        result = handler(data)
44	        if not isinstance(data, dict):
45	            return result
46	        field_names = cls.field_names
47	        if field_names is None:
48	            # Get all class field names and their potential aliases
49	            field_names = set()
50	            for field_name, field in cls.model_fields.items():
51	                field_names.add(field_name)
52	                if alias := getattr(field, "alias", None):
53	                    field_names.add(alias)
54	            cls.field_names = field_names
55	
56	        # Compare against both field names and aliases
57	        if any(k not in field_names for k in data):
58	            logger.warning(
59	                "The following fields were present in the request "
60	                "but ignored: %s",
61	                data.keys() - field_names,
62	            )
63	        return result

75	    async def create_completion(
76	        self,
77	        request: CompletionRequest,
78	        raw_request: Optional[Request] = None,
79	    ) -> Union[AsyncGenerator[str, None], CompletionResponse, ErrorResponse]:
80	        """Completion API similar to OpenAI's API.
81	
82	        See https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/completions/create
83	        for the API specification. This API mimics the OpenAI Completion API.
84	
85	        NOTE: Currently we do not support the following feature:
86	            - suffix (the language models we currently support do not support
87	            suffix)
88	        """
89	        error_check_ret = await self._check_model(request)
90	        if error_check_ret is not None:
91	            return error_check_ret
92	
93	        # If the engine is dead, raise the engine's DEAD_ERROR.
94	        # This is required for the streaming case, where we return a
95	        # success status before we actually start generating text :).
96	        if self.engine_client.errored:
97	            raise self.engine_client.dead_error
98	
99	        # Return error for unsupported features.
100	        if request.suffix is not None:
101	            return self.create_error_response(
102	                "suffix is not currently supported")
103	
104	        if request.echo and request.prompt_embeds is not None:
105	            return self.create_error_response(
106	                "Echo is unsupported with prompt embeds.")
107	
108	        request_id = f"cmpl-{self._base_request_id(raw_request)}"
109	        created_time = int(time.time())
110	
111	        request_metadata = RequestResponseMetadata(request_id=request_id)
112	        if raw_request:
113	            raw_request.state.request_metadata = request_metadata
114	
115	        try:
116	            (
117	                lora_request,
118	                prompt_adapter_request,
119	            ) = self._maybe_get_adapters(request)
120	
121	            tokenizer = await self.engine_client.get_tokenizer(lora_request)
122	
123	            request_prompts, engine_prompts = await self._preprocess_completion(
124	                request,
125	                tokenizer,
126	                request.prompt,
127	                truncate_prompt_tokens=request.truncate_prompt_tokens,
128	                add_special_tokens=request.add_special_tokens,
129	            )
130	        except ValueError as e:
131	            logger.exception("Error in preprocessing prompt inputs")
132	            return self.create_error_response(str(e))
133	        except TypeError as e:
134	            logger.exception("Error in preprocessing prompt inputs")
135	            return self.create_error_response(str(e))
136	        except RuntimeError as e:
137	            logger.exception("Error in preprocessing prompt inputs")
138	            return self.create_error_response(str(e))
139	        except jinja2.TemplateError as e:
140	            logger.exception("Error in preprocessing prompt inputs")
141	            return self.create_error_response(str(e))
142	
143	        # Schedule the request and get the result generator.
144	        generators: list[AsyncGenerator[RequestOutput, None]] = []
145	        try:
146	            for i, engine_prompt in enumerate(engine_prompts):
147	                sampling_params: Union[SamplingParams, BeamSearchParams]
148	                # Mypy does not infer that engine_prompt will have only one of
149	                # "prompt_token_ids" or "prompt_embeds" defined, and both of
150	                # these as Union[object, the expected type], where it infers
151	                # object if engine_prompt is a subclass of one of the
152	                # typeddicts that defines both keys. Worse, because of
153	                # https://github.com/python/mypy/issues/8586, mypy does not
154	                # infer the type of engine_prompt correctly because of the
155	                # enumerate. So we need an unnecessary cast here.
156	                engine_prompt = cast(Union[EmbedsPrompt, TokensPrompt],
157	                                     engine_prompt)
158	                if is_embeds_prompt(engine_prompt):
159	                    input_length = len(engine_prompt["prompt_embeds"])
160	                elif is_tokens_prompt(engine_prompt):
161	                    input_length = len(engine_prompt["prompt_token_ids"])
162	                else:
163	                    assert_never(engine_prompt)
164	
165	                if self.default_sampling_params is None:
166	                    self.default_sampling_params = {}
167	
168	                max_tokens = get_max_tokens(
169	                    max_model_len=self.max_model_len,
170	                    request=request,
171	                    input_length=input_length,
172	                    default_sampling_params=self.default_sampling_params)
173	
174	                if request.use_beam_search:
175	                    sampling_params = request.to_beam_search_params(
176	                        max_tokens, self.default_sampling_params)
177	                else:
178	                    sampling_params = request.to_sampling_params(
179	                        max_tokens, self.model_config.logits_processor_pattern,
180	                        self.default_sampling_params)
181	
182	                request_id_item = f"{request_id}-{i}"
183	
184	                self._log_inputs(request_id_item,
185	                                 request_prompts[i],
186	                                 params=sampling_params,
187	                                 lora_request=lora_request,
188	                                 prompt_adapter_request=prompt_adapter_request)
189	
190	                trace_headers = (None if raw_request is None else await
191	                                 self._get_trace_headers(raw_request.headers))
192	
193	                # Mypy inconsistently requires this second cast in different
194	                # environments. It shouldn't be necessary (redundant from above)
195	                # but pre-commit in CI fails without it.
196	                engine_prompt = cast(Union[EmbedsPrompt, TokensPrompt],
197	                                     engine_prompt)
198	                if isinstance(sampling_params, BeamSearchParams):
199	                    generator = self.engine_client.beam_search(
200	                        prompt=engine_prompt,
201	                        request_id=request_id,
202	                        params=sampling_params,
203	                        lora_request=lora_request,
204	                    )
205	                else:
206	                    generator = self.engine_client.generate(
207	                        engine_prompt,
208	                        sampling_params,
209	                        request_id_item,
210	                        lora_request=lora_request,
211	                        prompt_adapter_request=prompt_adapter_request,
212	                        trace_headers=trace_headers,
213	                        priority=request.priority,
214	                    )
215	
216	                generators.append(generator)
217	        except ValueError as e:
218	            # TODO: Use a vllm-specific Validation Error
219	            return self.create_error_response(str(e))
220	
221	        result_generator = merge_async_iterators(*generators)
222	
223	        model_name = self._get_model_name(request.model, lora_request)
224	        num_prompts = len(engine_prompts)
225	
226	        # Similar to the OpenAI API, when n != best_of, we do not stream the
227	        # results. Noting that best_of is only supported in V0. In addition,
228	        # we do not stream the results when use beam search.
229	        stream = (request.stream
230	                  and (request.best_of is None or request.n == request.best_of)
231	                  and not request.use_beam_search)
232	
233	        # Streaming response
234	        if stream:
235	            return self.completion_stream_generator(
236	                request,
237	                request_prompts,
238	                result_generator,
239	                request_id,
240	                created_time,
241	                model_name,
242	                num_prompts=num_prompts,
243	                tokenizer=tokenizer,
244	                request_metadata=request_metadata,
245	                enable_force_include_usage=self.enable_force_include_usage)
246	
247	        # Non-streaming response
248	        final_res_batch: list[Optional[RequestOutput]] = [None] * num_prompts
249	        try:
250	            async for i, res in result_generator:
251	                final_res_batch[i] = res
252	
253	            for i, final_res in enumerate(final_res_batch):
254	                assert final_res is not None
255	
256	                # The output should contain the input text
257	                # We did not pass it into vLLM engine to avoid being redundant
258	                # with the inputs token IDs
259	                if final_res.prompt is None:
260	                    request_prompt = request_prompts[i]
261	                    if is_text_tokens_prompt(request_prompt):
262	                        final_res.prompt = request_prompt["prompt"]
263	                    else:
264	                        final_res.prompt = None
265	
266	            final_res_batch_checked = cast(list[RequestOutput],
267	                                           final_res_batch)
268	
269	            response = self.request_output_to_completion_response(
270	                final_res_batch_checked,
271	                request,
272	                request_id,
273	                created_time,
274	                model_name,
275	                tokenizer,
276	                request_metadata,
277	            )
278	        except asyncio.CancelledError:
279	            return self.create_error_response("Client disconnected")
280	        except ValueError as e:
281	            # TODO: Use a vllm-specific Validation Error
282	            return self.create_error_response(str(e))
283	
284	        # When user requests streaming but we don't stream, we still need to
285	        # return a streaming response with a single event.
286	        if request.stream:
287	            response_json = response.model_dump_json()
288	
289	            async def fake_stream_generator() -> AsyncGenerator[str, None]:
290	                yield f"data: {response_json}\n\n"
291	                yield "data: [DONE]\n\n"
292	
293	            return fake_stream_generator()
294	
295	        return response

300	        request_prompts: list[Union[TextTokensPrompt,

151	        Args:
152	          logprobs: (num tokens) x (vocab) tensor
153	          num_logprobs: minimum number of logprobs to
154	                        retain per token
155	          token_ids: prompt tokens (if prompt logprobs)
156	                     or sampled tokens (if sampled
157	                     logprobs); 1D token ID tensor
158	                     with (num tokens) elements
159	                     Must be int64.
160	
161	        Returns:
162	          Top-k int indices tensor, (num tokens) x (num_logprobs + 1)
163	          Top-k float logprobs tensor, (num tokens) x (num_logprobs + 1)
164	          Sampled token rank tensor, (num tokens)
165	        """
166	        assert token_ids.dtype == torch.int64
167	        # Find the topK values.
168	        topk_logprobs, topk_indices = torch.topk(logprobs,
169	                                                 num_logprobs,
170	                                                 dim=-1)
171	
172	        # Get with the logprob of the prompt or sampled token.
173	        token_ids = token_ids.unsqueeze(-1)
174	        token_logprobs = logprobs.gather(-1, token_ids)
175	
176	        # Compute the ranks of the actual token.
177	        token_ranks = (logprobs >= token_logprobs).sum(-1)
178	
179	        # Concatenate together with the topk.
180	        indices = torch.cat((token_ids, topk_indices), dim=1)
181	        logprobs = torch.cat((token_logprobs, topk_logprobs), dim=1)
182	
183	        # Use int32 to reduce the tensor size.
184	        indices = indices.to(torch.int32)
185	
186	        return LogprobsTensors(indices, logprobs, token_ranks)

188	    def apply_penalties(
189	        self,
190	        logits: torch.Tensor,
191	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
192	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
193	        if not sampling_metadata.no_penalties:
194	            assert sampling_metadata.prompt_token_ids is not None
195	            logits = apply_all_penalties(
196	                logits,
197	                sampling_metadata.prompt_token_ids,
198	                sampling_metadata.presence_penalties,
199	                sampling_metadata.frequency_penalties,
200	                sampling_metadata.repetition_penalties,
201	                sampling_metadata.output_token_ids,
202	            )
203	        return logits
204	
205	    def apply_allowed_token_ids(
206	        self,
207	        logits: torch.Tensor,
208	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
209	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
210	        if sampling_metadata.allowed_token_ids_mask is not None:
211	            logits.masked_fill_(sampling_metadata.allowed_token_ids_mask,
212	                                float("-inf"))
213	        return logits
214	
215	    def apply_bad_words(
216	        self,
217	        logits: torch.Tensor,

219	    ) -> torch.Tensor:

224	                sampling_metadata.output_token_ids,
225	            )

4	import torch
5	
6	from vllm.model_executor.layers.utils import apply_penalties
7	from vllm.utils import is_pin_memory_available, make_tensor_with_pad
8	
9	
10	def apply_all_penalties(
11	    logits: torch.Tensor,
12	    prompt_token_ids: torch.Tensor,
13	    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor,
14	    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor,
15	    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor,
16	    output_token_ids: list[list[int]],
17	) -> torch.Tensor:
18	    """
19	    Applies presence, frequency and repetition penalties to the logits.
20	    """
21	    _, vocab_size = logits.shape
22	    output_tokens_t = _convert_to_tensors(output_token_ids, vocab_size,
23	                                          logits.device)
24	    return apply_penalties(logits, prompt_token_ids, output_tokens_t,
25	                           presence_penalties, frequency_penalties,
26	                           repetition_penalties)
27	
28	
29	def _convert_to_tensors(output_token_ids: list[list[int]], vocab_size: int,
30	                        device: torch.device) -> torch.Tensor:
31	    """
32	    Convert the different list data structures to tensors.
33	    """
34	    output_tokens_tensor = make_tensor_with_pad(
35	        output_token_ids,
36	        # Use the value of vocab_size as a pad since we don't have a
37	        # token_id of this value.
38	        pad=vocab_size,
39	        device="cpu",
40	        dtype=torch.int64,
41	        pin_memory=is_pin_memory_available(),
42	    )
43	    return output_tokens_tensor.to(device, non_blocking=True)
44

743	    # https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/completions/create

23-    bin_counts.scatter_add_(1, tokens, torch.ones_like(tokens))

30:def apply_penalties(logits: torch.Tensor, prompt_tokens_tensor: torch.Tensor,
31-                    output_tokens_tensor: torch.Tensor,
32-                    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor,
33-                    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor,
34-                    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
35-    """
36-    Applies penalties in place to the logits tensor
37-    logits : The input logits tensor of shape [num_seqs, vocab_size]
38-    prompt_tokens_tensor: A tensor containing the prompt tokens. The prompts 
39-        are padded to the maximum prompt length within the batch using 
40-        `vocab_size` as the padding value. The value `vocab_size` is used 
41-        for padding because it does not correspond to any valid token ID 
42-        in the vocabulary.
43-    output_tokens_tensor: The output tokens tensor.
44-    presence_penalties: The presence penalties of shape (num_seqs, )
45-    frequency_penalties: The frequency penalties of shape (num_seqs, )
46-    repetition_penalties: The repetition penalties of shape (num_seqs, )
47-    """
48-    num_seqs, vocab_size = logits.shape
49-    _, prompt_mask = get_token_bin_counts_and_mask(prompt_tokens_tensor,
50-                                                   vocab_size, num_seqs)
51-    output_bin_counts, output_mask = get_token_bin_counts_and_mask(
52-        output_tokens_tensor, vocab_size, num_seqs)
53-
54-    # Apply repetition penalties as a custom op
55-    from vllm._custom_ops import apply_repetition_penalties
56-    apply_repetition_penalties(logits, prompt_mask, output_mask,
57-                               repetition_penalties)
58-
59-    # We follow the definition in OpenAI API.
60-    # Refer to https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/parameter-details
61-    logits -= frequency_penalties.unsqueeze(dim=1) * output_bin_counts
62-    logits -= presence_penalties.unsqueeze(dim=1) * output_mask
63-    return logits

398	def _apply_top_k_top_p(
399	    logits: torch.Tensor,
400	    p: torch.Tensor,
401	    k: torch.Tensor,
402	) -> torch.Tensor:
403	    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
404	
405	    # Apply top-k.
406	    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
407	    # Get all the top_k values.
408	    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
409	    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
410	    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
411	
412	    # Apply top-p.
413	    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
414	    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
415	    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
416	    # at least one
417	    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
418	    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
419	
420	    # Re-sort the probabilities.
421	    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1,
422	                                                    index=logits_idx,

183	    The structure of the logits tensor is coupled with the seq_groups in
184	    sampling_metadata. Typically, each sequence in each seq_group has one row in
185	    logits for the next token to be sampled; however, for a seq_group with a
186	    prompt request with the prompt_logprobs sampling parameter, there are rows
187	    in logits for each token in the input prompt.

398	def _apply_top_k_top_p(
399	    logits: torch.Tensor,
400	    p: torch.Tensor,
401	    k: torch.Tensor,
402	) -> torch.Tensor:
403	    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
404	
405	    # Apply top-k.
406	    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
407	    # Get all the top_k values.
408	    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
409	    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
410	    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
411	
412	    # Apply top-p.
413	    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
414	    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
415	    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
416	    # at least one
417	    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
418	    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
419	
420	    # Re-sort the probabilities.
421	    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1,
422	                                                    index=logits_idx,
423	                                                    src=logits_sort)
424	    return logits
425

427	def _apply_min_p(
428	    logits: torch.Tensor,
429	    min_p: torch.Tensor,
430	) -> torch.Tensor:
431	    """
432	    Adapted from
433	    https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui/blob/3146124ec01f02c8fb1650a6517cf1b60b537aaf/modules/sampler_hijack.py#L16C17-L16C17
434	    """
435	    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
436	    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
437	    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
438	    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
439	    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
440	
441	    return logits

452	        samples: (num_selected_samples,) A tensor of samples. The length of
453	            samples could be smaller than selected_seq_groups if

455	    Returns:
456	        Tuple of (next_token_ids, parent_ids). The length of returned list is
457	        same as the length of selected_seq_groups. If the corresponding
458	        seq_group has do_sample=False, tuple contains ([], [])
459	    """
460	    samples_lst = samples.tolist()
461	    sample_idx = 0
462	    results: SampleResultType = []
463	    for seq_group in selected_seq_groups:
464	        if not seq_group.do_sample:
465	            results.append(([], []))
466	            continue
467	
468	        seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
469	        num_parent_seqs = len(seq_ids)
470	        assert num_parent_seqs == 1, (
471	            "Greedy sampling should have only one seq.")
472	        parent_ids = list(range(num_parent_seqs))
473	        next_token_ids = [samples_lst[sample_idx]]
474	        results.append((next_token_ids, parent_ids))
475	        sample_idx += num_parent_seqs
476	    return results

Perfect! Now I have all the information needed. Let me compile a comprehensive analysis document:

## VLLM Sampling Implementation Analysis

Based on my exploration of the vLLM codebase, here's a comprehensive overview of how sampling is implemented at the logits/probability level:

### KEY FILES FOUND

#### 1. **Core Sampling Parameter Structures**
- **`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775332077308-imdd0b/vllm/sampling_params.py`** (Lines 1-603)
  - Defines `SamplingParams` dataclass with: `top_p`, `top_k`, `temperature`, penalties (presence, frequency, repetition), `min_p`
  - Lines 212-214: Default values - `top_p=1.0`, `top_k=0`, `min_p=0.0`
  - Lines 417-428: Parameter validation for `top_p` (must be in (0, 1]) and `top_k` (must be >= 0 or -1)

#### 2. **Sampling Metadata & Tensor Management**
- **`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775332077308-imdd0b/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`** (Lines 1-598)
  - **`SamplingTensors` dataclass** (Lines 372-598): Core data structure carrying batched sampling parameters
    - Lines 376-378: `top_ps`, `top_ks` as torch.Tensor fields
    - Lines 386-495: `from_sampling_metadata()` classmethod that extracts top_p/top_k from `SamplingParams`
    - Lines 426-428: Logic to set `do_top_p_top_k = True` if `top_p < 1.0 - eps` OR `top_k != vocab_size`
    - Lines 415-420: Top_k clamping: `top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)` then `top_k = vocab_size if top_k < 1 else top_k`
  - **`SamplingMetadata` dataclass** (Lines 97-189): Routes sampling parameters to logits processing
    - Lines 141-146: Fields: `seq_groups`, `selected_token_indices`, `categorized_sample_indices`

#### 3. **V0 Sampler (Legacy) - Top-K & Top-P Implementation**
- **`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775332077308-imdd0b/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py`** (Lines 1-636)
  - **Lines 284-286**: When flashinfer is not available:
    ```python
    if do_top_p_top_k and flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling is None:
        logits = _apply_top_k_top_p(logits, sampling_tensors.top_ps,
                                    sampling_tensors.top_ks)
    ```
  - **`_apply_top_k_top_p()` function** (Lines 398-424) - EXACT TENSOR OPERATIONS:
    - Line 403: `logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)` - Sort in ascending order
    - **Top-K filtering** (Lines 405-410):
      ```python
      top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # Line 406: Calculate cutoff index
      top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))  # Line 408: Get cutoff value
      ```

5. Temperature scaling (sampler.py, line 282)
6. **Top-K/Top-P filtering** (sampler.py, lines 284-286 → 398-424 OR FlashInfer)

**V1 Pipeline:**
1. Forward pass → logits
2. Apply allowed_token_ids (sampler.py, lines 43)
3. Apply bad_words (sampler.py, lines 45)
4. Apply logits processors (sampler.py, lines 48-49)
5. Apply penalties (sampler.py, line 52)
6. **Top-K/Top-P via `topk_topp_sampler`** (sampler.py, lines 121-126)
7. Multinomial sampling (not shown in excerpt but implicit)

### EXACT TENSOR OPERATIONS FOR TOP-K & TOP-P

**Top-K Filtering** (both V0 and V1 use same logic):
- Input: `logits` [batch, vocab], `k` [batch]
- Sort ascending: `logits_sort = sort(logits)`
- Get k-th largest value index: `k_idx = vocab_size - k`
- Get threshold: `threshold = logits_sort.gather(1, k_idx.unsqueeze(1))`
- Mask: `mask = logits_sort < threshold` (keep top-k, mask rest)
- Apply: `logits_sort.masked_fill_(mask, -inf)`

**Top-P Filtering** (both V0 and V1):
- Input: `logits` [batch, vocab], `p` [batch] (e.g., 0.9)
- Convert to probs: `probs = softmax(logits)`
- Cumulative sum: `cum_probs = cumsum(probs)` (in sorted order)
- Mask: `mask = cum_probs <= 1 - p` (filter low-probability tail)
- Guarantee one token: `mask[:, -1] = False`
- Apply: `logits.masked_fill_(mask, -inf)`
- Final softmax & sample from remaining valid tokens

---

### PARAMETER ACCESS & FLOW

**How top_p and top_k reach tensor ops:**
1. User provides `SamplingParams(top_p=0.9, top_k=40)` per request
2. Grouped into `SamplingMetadata.seq_groups[i].sampling_params`
3. Extracted to `SamplingTensors.top_ps` and `SamplingTensors.top_ks` tensors (shape: [batch])
4. Passed directly to `_apply_top_k_top_p(logits, top_ps_tensor, top_ks_tensor)`
5. Per-row masking in matrix ops ensures per-sequence parameters apply correctly

**Special cases:**
- `top_k=0` or `< 1` → treated as "no top-k filtering" (set to vocab_size)
- `top_p=1.0` → no top-p filtering
- `temperature < eps` → greedy sampling (uses `argmax` instead of sampling)

---

This is a read-only analysis. All files are left untouched. No modifications, edits, or new files created.

## Comprehensive Report: Sampling Parameters Flow in vLLM

I've thoroughly explored the vLLM codebase and traced how sampling parameters flow from the high-level API down to the actual sampling tensors. Here's my detailed findings:

---

### 1. **Entry Point: SamplingParams Class**
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775332077308-imdd0b/vllm/sampling_params.py`

- **Lines 118-245:** Core `SamplingParams` msgspec.Struct definition
  - `top_p: float = 1.0` (line 212)
  - `top_k: int = 0` (line 213)
  - `temperature: float = 1.0` (line 211)
  - Also stores: `presence_penalty`, `frequency_penalty`, `repetition_penalty`, `min_p`, `seed`, `stop`, etc.
  - Validation in `_verify_args()` (lines 387-462) validates `top_p`, `top_k`, and other parameters
  - **Lines 251-325:** `from_optional()` factory method creates instances from optional parameters
  - **Lines 327-385:** `__post_init__()` applies special logic: when temperature < epsilon, sets `top_p=1.0`, `top_k=0` (lines 377-381)

---

### 2. **API Layer: OpenAI-Compatible Mapping**
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775332077308-imdd0b/vllm/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py`

- **Lines 741-981:** `CompletionRequest` class with OpenAI API fields
  - **Line 761:** `top_p: Optional[float] = None`
  - **Line 766:** `top_k: Optional[int] = None`
  - **Line 760:** `temperature: Optional[float] = None`
  - **Lines 867-873:** `_DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS` dict defines defaults:
    ```python
    "repetition_penalty": 1.0,
    "temperature": 1.0,
    "top_p": 1.0,
    "top_k": 0,
    "min_p": 0.0,
    ```

- **Lines 897-981:** `to_sampling_params()` method converts API request to SamplingParams

  - **Line 916-924:** Handles `top_p` and `top_k` default resolution
  ```python
  if (top_p := self.top_p) is None:
      top_p = default_sampling_params.get(
          "top_p", self._DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS["top_p"])
  if (top_k := self.top_k) is None:
      top_k = default_sampling_params.get(
          "top_k", self._DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS["top_k"])
  ```
  - **Lines 951-981:** Returns `SamplingParams.from_optional()` with all parameters threaded through

### 3. **Request Processing Flow**
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775332077308-imdd0b/vllm/entrypoints/openai/serving_completion.py`

  - **Line 1030:** `sampling_params: Optional[SamplingParams]` - carries the params through the pipeline

  - **Line 30:** `sampling_params: SamplingParams` - carries params for each sequence group

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775332077308-imdd0b/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`

  - **Lines 376-384:** GPU tensor fields:
    ```python
    top_ps: torch.Tensor  # Top-p values for each token
    top_ks: torch.Tensor  # Top-k values for each token
    temperatures: torch.Tensor
    ```

    - **Line 410:** `sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params`
    - **Line 415:** `top_p = sampling_params.top_p` (line 415)
    - **Line 419:** `top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)` (line 419)
    - **Line 411:** `temperature = sampling_params.temperature` (line 411)
    - **Lines 443-460:** Builds lists of values for each token in batch:
      ```python

      top_ks += [top_k] * prefill_len
      temperatures += [temperature] * sample_lens
      ```
    - **Lines 426-434:** Sets flags `do_top_p_top_k`, `do_min_p` based on parameter values

  - **Lines 497-597:** `from_lists()` class method - **TENSOR CONVERSION**
    - **Lines 545-550:** Creates torch tensors from Python lists:
    ```python
    top_ps_t = torch.tensor(
        top_ps,
        device="cpu",
        dtype=dtype,
        pin_memory=pin_memory,
    )
    top_ks_t = torch.tensor(
        top_ks,
        device="cpu",
        dtype=torch.int,
        pin_memory=pin_memory,
    )
    ```
    - **Lines 585-597:** Transfers to target device with non-blocking async copy

---

### 7. **Sampler Layer: Using the Tensors**
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775332077308-imdd0b/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py`

- **Lines 199-223:** `_init_sampling_tensors()` method
  - **Line 217:** `SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata()` called to build tensors
  - Stores `self._sampling_tensors` for use in forward pass

- **Lines 225-334:** `forward()` method
  - **Line 282:** Applies temperature: `logits.div_(sampling_tensors.temperatures.unsqueeze(dim=1))`
  - **Lines 285-286:** Applies top-k/top-p filtering:
    ```python

                                sampling_tensors.top_ks)
    ```

- **Lines 398-424:** `_apply_top_k_top_p()` function - **ACTUAL SAMPLING LOGIC**
  - **Line 406:** `top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)` - uses top_k tensor
  - **Line 415:** `top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)` - uses top_p tensor

---

### 8. **Distributed Worker Serialization**
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775332077308-imdd0b/vllm/worker/model_runner_base.py`

- **Lines 60-78:** `_init_sampling_metadata_from_tensor_dict()` - **DESERIALIZATION**
  - Reconstructs SamplingMetadata from tensor dictionary (for distributed workers)

- **Lines 81-91:** `_add_sampling_metadata_broadcastable_dict()` - **SERIALIZATION**
  - **Lines 88-90:** Extracts broadcastable fields from SamplingMetadata:
    ```python
    if sampling_metadata is not None:
        tensor_dict["selected_token_indices"] = (
            sampling_metadata.selected_token_indices)
    ```

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775332077308-imdd0b/vllm/worker/model_runner.py`

- **Lines 1742-1747:** `_prepare_model_runner_execution_inputs()`
  - **Line 1742:** `SamplingMetadata.prepare()` reconstructs full metadata from scheduler output
  - SamplingParams come from `SequenceGroupMetadata` which was populated by scheduler

---

### 9. **End-to-End Flow Summary**

```
OpenAI API Request (top_p, top_k, temperature)
    ↓
CompletionRequest.to_sampling_params() [protocol.py:897-981]
    ↓
SamplingParams instance with top_p/top_k/temperature [sampling_params.py:118-245]

Scheduler routes to workers with SamplingParams

_apply_top_k_top_p() applies actual filtering [sampler.py:398-424]
```

```
PyTorch tensors (top_ps, top_ks, temperatures) [sampling_metadata.py:545-597]
    ↓
Sampler.forward() uses tensors for logit manipulation [sampler.py:225-334]
    ↓
_apply_top_k_top_p() applies actual filtering [sampler.py:398-424]
    ↓
Sample tokens using modified logits
```

---

### 10. **Key Line Numbers for Reference**

| Component | File | Lines | Purpose |

This is the complete parameter flow for sampling in vLLM, with exact line numbers showing the threading of `top_p`, `top_k`, and other sampling parameters from the API down to the GPU tensors used in actual token sampling.

class SamplingParams(
        msgspec.Struct,
        omit_defaults=True,  # type: ignore[call-arg]
        # required for @cached_property.
        dict=True):  # type: ignore[call-arg]
    """Sampling parameters for text generation.

    Overall, we follow the sampling parameters from the OpenAI text completion
    API (https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/completions/create).
    In addition, we support beam search, which is not supported by OpenAI.

    Args:
        n: Number of output sequences to return for the given prompt.
        best_of: Number of output sequences that are generated from the prompt.
            From these `best_of` sequences, the top `n` sequences are returned.
            `best_of` must be greater than or equal to `n`. By default,
            `best_of` is set to `n`. Warning, this is only supported in V0.
        presence_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether they
            appear in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the model
            to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to repeat
            tokens.
        frequency_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on their
            frequency in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the
            model to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to
            repeat tokens.
        repetition_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether
            they appear in the prompt and the generated text so far. Values > 1
            encourage the model to use new tokens, while values < 1 encourage
            the model to repeat tokens.
        temperature: Float that controls the randomness of the sampling. Lower
            values make the model more deterministic, while higher values make
            the model more random. Zero means greedy sampling.
        top_p: Float that controls the cumulative probability of the top tokens
            to consider. Must be in (0, 1]. Set to 1 to consider all tokens.
        top_k: Integer that controls the number of top tokens to consider. Set
            to 0 (or -1) to consider all tokens.
        min_p: Float that represents the minimum probability for a token to be
            considered, relative to the probability of the most likely token.
            Must be in [0, 1]. Set to 0 to disable this.
        seed: Random seed to use for the generation.
        stop: list of strings that stop the generation when they are generated.
            The returned output will not contain the stop strings.
        stop_token_ids: list of tokens that stop the generation when they are
            generated. The returned output will contain the stop tokens unless
            the stop tokens are special tokens.
        bad_words: list of words that are not allowed to be generated.
            More precisely, only the last token of a corresponding
            token sequence is not allowed when the next generated token
            can complete the sequence.
        include_stop_str_in_output: Whether to include the stop strings in
            output text. Defaults to False.
        ignore_eos: Whether to ignore the EOS token and continue generating
            tokens after the EOS token is generated.
        max_tokens: Maximum number of tokens to generate per output sequence.
        min_tokens: Minimum number of tokens to generate per output sequence
            before EOS or stop_token_ids can be generated
        logprobs: Number of log probabilities to return per output token.
            When set to None, no probability is returned. If set to a non-None
            value, the result includes the log probabilities of the specified
            number of most likely tokens, as well as the chosen tokens.
            Note that the implementation follows the OpenAI API: The API will
            always return the log probability of the sampled token, so there
            may be up to `logprobs+1` elements in the response.
        prompt_logprobs: Number of log probabilities to return per prompt token.
        detokenize: Whether to detokenize the output. Defaults to True.
        skip_special_tokens: Whether to skip special tokens in the output.
        spaces_between_special_tokens: Whether to add spaces between special
            tokens in the output.  Defaults to True.
        logits_processors: list of functions that modify logits based on
            previously generated tokens, and optionally prompt tokens as
            a first argument.
        truncate_prompt_tokens: If set to -1, will use the truncation size
            supported by the model. If set to an integer k, will use only
            the last k tokens from the prompt (i.e., left truncation).
            Defaults to None (i.e., no truncation).
        guided_decoding: If provided, the engine will construct a guided
            decoding logits processor from these parameters. Defaults to None.
        logit_bias: If provided, the engine will construct a logits processor
            that applies these logit biases. Defaults to None.
        allowed_token_ids: If provided, the engine will construct a logits
            processor which only retains scores for the given token ids.
            Defaults to None.
        extra_args: Arbitrary additional args, that can be used by custom
            sampling implementations, plugins, etc. Not used by any in-tree
            sampling implementations.
    """

    n: int = 1
    best_of: Optional[int] = None
    _real_n: Optional[int] = None
    presence_penalty: float = 0.0
    frequency_penalty: float = 0.0
    repetition_penalty: float = 1.0
    temperature: float = 1.0
    top_p: float = 1.0
    top_k: int = 0
    min_p: float = 0.0
    seed: Optional[int] = None
    stop: Optional[Union[str, list[str]]] = None
    stop_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None
    ignore_eos: bool = False
    max_tokens: Optional[int] = 16
    min_tokens: int = 0
    logprobs: Optional[int] = None
    prompt_logprobs: Optional[int] = None
    # NOTE: This parameter is only exposed at the engine level for now.
    # It is not exposed in the OpenAI API server, as the OpenAI API does
    # not support returning only a list of token IDs.
    detokenize: bool = True
    skip_special_tokens: bool = True
    spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True
    # Optional[list[LogitsProcessor]] type. We use Any here because
    # Optional[list[LogitsProcessor]] type is not supported by msgspec.
    logits_processors: Optional[Any] = None
    include_stop_str_in_output: bool = False
    truncate_prompt_tokens: Optional[Annotated[int, msgspec.Meta(ge=1)]] = None
    output_kind: RequestOutputKind = RequestOutputKind.CUMULATIVE

    # The below fields are not supposed to be used as an input.
    # They are set in post_init.
    output_text_buffer_length: int = 0
    _all_stop_token_ids: set[int] = msgspec.field(default_factory=set)

    # Fields used to construct logits processors
    guided_decoding: Optional[GuidedDecodingParams] = None
    logit_bias: Optional[dict[int, float]] = None
    allowed_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None
    extra_args: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None

    # Fields used for bad words
    bad_words: Optional[list[str]] = None
    _bad_words_token_ids: Optional[list[list[int]]] = None

    @staticmethod
    def from_optional(
        n: Optional[int] = 1,
        best_of: Optional[int] = None,
        presence_penalty: Optional[float] = 0.0,
        frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = 0.0,
        repetition_penalty: Optional[float] = 1.0,
        temperature: Optional[float] = 1.0,
        top_p: Optional[float] = 1.0,
        top_k: int = 0,
        min_p: float = 0.0,
        seed: Optional[int] = None,
        stop: Optional[Union[str, list[str]]] = None,
        stop_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None,
        bad_words: Optional[list[str]] = None,
        include_stop_str_in_output: bool = False,
        ignore_eos: bool = False,
        max_tokens: Optional[int] = 16,
        min_tokens: int = 0,
        logprobs: Optional[int] = None,
        prompt_logprobs: Optional[int] = None,
        detokenize: bool = True,
        skip_special_tokens: bool = True,
        spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True,
        logits_processors: Optional[list[LogitsProcessor]] = None,
        truncate_prompt_tokens: Optional[Annotated[int,
                                                   msgspec.Meta(ge=1)]] = None,
        output_kind: RequestOutputKind = RequestOutputKind.CUMULATIVE,
        guided_decoding: Optional[GuidedDecodingParams] = None,
        logit_bias: Optional[Union[dict[int, float], dict[str, float]]] = None,
        allowed_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None,
        extra_args: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None,
    ) -> "SamplingParams":
        if logit_bias is not None:
            # Convert token_id to integer
            # Clamp the bias between -100 and 100 per OpenAI API spec
            logit_bias = {
                int(token): min(100.0, max(-100.0, bias))
                for token, bias in logit_bias.items()
            }

        return SamplingParams(
            n=1 if n is None else n,
            best_of=best_of,
            presence_penalty=0.0
            if presence_penalty is None else presence_penalty,
            frequency_penalty=0.0
            if frequency_penalty is None else frequency_penalty,
            repetition_penalty=1.0
            if repetition_penalty is None else repetition_penalty,
            temperature=1.0 if temperature is None else temperature,
            top_p=1.0 if top_p is None else top_p,
            top_k=top_k,
            min_p=min_p,
            seed=seed,
            stop=stop,
            stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids,
            bad_words=bad_words,
            include_stop_str_in_output=include_stop_str_in_output,
            ignore_eos=ignore_eos,
            max_tokens=max_tokens,
            min_tokens=min_tokens,
            logprobs=logprobs,
            prompt_logprobs=prompt_logprobs,
            detokenize=detokenize,
            skip_special_tokens=skip_special_tokens,
            spaces_between_special_tokens=spaces_between_special_tokens,
            logits_processors=logits_processors,
            truncate_prompt_tokens=truncate_prompt_tokens,
            output_kind=output_kind,
            guided_decoding=guided_decoding,
            logit_bias=logit_bias,
            allowed_token_ids=allowed_token_ids,
            extra_args=extra_args,
        )

    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
        # how we deal with `best_of``:
        # if `best_of`` is not set, we default to `n`;
        # if `best_of`` is set, we set `n`` to `best_of`,
        # and set `_real_n`` to the original `n`.
        # when we return the result, we will check
        # if we need to return `n` or `_real_n` results
        if self.best_of:
            if self.best_of < self.n:
                raise ValueError(
                    f"best_of must be greater than or equal to n, "
                    f"got n={self.n} and best_of={self.best_of}.")
            if not self._real_n:
                self._real_n = self.n
                self.n = self.best_of

        if 0 < self.temperature < _MAX_TEMP:
            logger.warning(
                "temperature %s is less than %s, which may cause numerical "
                "errors nan or inf in tensors. We have maxed it out to %s.",
                self.temperature, _MAX_TEMP, _MAX_TEMP)
            self.temperature = max(self.temperature, _MAX_TEMP)

        if self.seed == -1:
            self.seed = None

        if self.stop is None:
            self.stop = []
        elif isinstance(self.stop, str):
            self.stop = [self.stop]

        if self.stop_token_ids is None:
            self.stop_token_ids = []

        if self.bad_words is None:
            self.bad_words = []

        if self.logprobs is True:
            self.logprobs = 1

        if self.prompt_logprobs is True:
            self.prompt_logprobs = 1

        # Number of characters to hold back for stop string evaluation
        # until sequence is finished.
        if self.stop and not self.include_stop_str_in_output:
            self.output_text_buffer_length = max(len(s) for s in self.stop) - 1

        self._verify_args()

        if self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
            # Zero temperature means greedy sampling.
            self.top_p = 1.0
            self.top_k = 0
            self.min_p = 0.0
            self._verify_greedy_sampling()

        # eos_token_id is added to this by the engine
        self._all_stop_token_ids.update(self.stop_token_ids)

    def _verify_args(self) -> None:
        if not isinstance(self.n, int):
            raise ValueError(f"n must be an int, but is of "
                             f"type {type(self.n)}")
        if self.n < 1:
            raise ValueError(f"n must be at least 1, got {self.n}.")
        if self.best_of is not None:
            if not isinstance(self.best_of, int):
                raise ValueError(
                    f"best_of must be an integer, got {type(self.best_of)}")
            if self.best_of < 1:
                raise ValueError(
                    f"best_of must be at least 1, got {self.best_of}")
            if self.best_of < self.n:
                raise ValueError(
                    f"best_of must be greater than or equal to n, "
                    f"got n={self.n} and best_of={self.best_of}.")
        if not -2.0 <= self.presence_penalty <= 2.0:
            raise ValueError("presence_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
                             f"{self.presence_penalty}.")
        if not -2.0 <= self.frequency_penalty <= 2.0:
            raise ValueError("frequency_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
                             f"{self.frequency_penalty}.")
        if self.repetition_penalty <= 0.0:
            raise ValueError(
                "repetition_penalty must be greater than zero, got "
                f"{self.repetition_penalty}.")
        if self.temperature < 0.0:
            raise ValueError(
                f"temperature must be non-negative, got {self.temperature}.")
        if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
            raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
        # quietly accept -1 as disabled, but prefer 0
        if self.top_k < -1:
            raise ValueError(f"top_k must be 0 (disable), or at least 1, "
                             f"got {self.top_k}.")
        if not isinstance(self.top_k, int):
            raise TypeError(
                f"top_k must be an integer, got {type(self.top_k).__name__}")
        if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0:
            raise ValueError("min_p must be in [0, 1], got "
                             f"{self.min_p}.")
        if self.max_tokens is not None and self.max_tokens < 1:
            raise ValueError(
                f"max_tokens must be at least 1, got {self.max_tokens}.")
        if self.min_tokens < 0:
            raise ValueError(f"min_tokens must be greater than or equal to 0, "
                             f"got {self.min_tokens}.")
        if self.max_tokens is not None and self.min_tokens > self.max_tokens:
            raise ValueError(
                f"min_tokens must be less than or equal to "
                f"max_tokens={self.max_tokens}, got {self.min_tokens}.")
        if self.logprobs is not None and self.logprobs < 0:
            raise ValueError(
                f"logprobs must be non-negative, got {self.logprobs}.")
        if self.prompt_logprobs is not None and self.prompt_logprobs < 0:
            raise ValueError(f"prompt_logprobs must be non-negative, got "
                             f"{self.prompt_logprobs}.")
        if (self.truncate_prompt_tokens is not None
                and self.truncate_prompt_tokens < 1):
            raise ValueError(f"truncate_prompt_tokens must be >= 1, "
                             f"got {self.truncate_prompt_tokens}")
        assert isinstance(self.stop_token_ids, list)
        if not all(isinstance(st_id, int) for st_id in self.stop_token_ids):
            raise ValueError(f"stop_token_ids must contain only integers, "
                             f"got {self.stop_token_ids}.")
        assert isinstance(self.stop, list)
        if any(not stop_str for stop_str in self.stop):
            raise ValueError("stop cannot contain an empty string.")

@dataclass
class SamplingTensors:
    """Tensors for sampling."""

    temperatures: torch.Tensor
    top_ps: torch.Tensor
    top_ks: torch.Tensor
    min_ps: torch.Tensor
    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor
    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor
    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor
    prompt_tokens: torch.Tensor
    output_tokens: torch.Tensor

    @classmethod
    def from_sampling_metadata(
        cls,
        sampling_metadata: "SamplingMetadata",
        vocab_size: int,
        device: torch.device,
        dtype: torch.dtype,
    ) -> tuple["SamplingTensors", bool, bool, bool]:
        prompt_tokens: list[array] = []
        output_tokens: list[array] = []
        top_ks: list[int] = []
        temperatures: list[float] = []
        top_ps: list[float] = []
        min_ps: list[float] = []
        presence_penalties: list[float] = []
        frequency_penalties: list[float] = []
        repetition_penalties: list[float] = []
        do_penalties = False
        do_top_p_top_k = False
        do_min_p = False

        assert sampling_metadata.seq_groups is not None
        for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
            seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
            sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
            temperature = sampling_params.temperature
            p = sampling_params.presence_penalty
            f = sampling_params.frequency_penalty
            r = sampling_params.repetition_penalty
            top_p = sampling_params.top_p
            min_p = sampling_params.min_p

            # k should not be greater than the vocab size.
            top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)
            top_k = vocab_size if top_k < 1 else top_k
            if temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
                # NOTE: Zero temperature means deterministic sampling
                # (i.e., greedy sampling or beam search).
                # Set the temperature to 1 to avoid division by zero.
                temperature = 1.0
            if not do_top_p_top_k and (top_p < 1.0 - _SAMPLING_EPS
                                       or top_k != vocab_size):
                do_top_p_top_k = True
            if not do_min_p and min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS:
                do_min_p = True
            if not do_penalties and (abs(p) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
                                     or abs(f) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
                                     or abs(r - 1.0) >= _SAMPLING_EPS):
                do_penalties = True

            is_prompt = seq_group.is_prompt
            if is_prompt and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
                # For tokens in the prompt that we only need to get
                # their logprobs
                query_len = seq_group.query_len
                assert query_len is not None
                prefill_len = len(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
                temperatures += [temperature] * prefill_len
                top_ps += [top_p] * prefill_len
                top_ks += [top_k] * prefill_len
                min_ps += [min_p] * prefill_len
                presence_penalties += [0] * prefill_len
                frequency_penalties += [0] * prefill_len
                repetition_penalties += [1] * prefill_len

            if seq_group.do_sample:
                sample_lens = len(seq_group.sample_indices)
                assert sample_lens >= len(seq_ids)
                temperatures += [temperature] * sample_lens
                top_ps += [top_p] * sample_lens
                top_ks += [top_k] * sample_lens
                min_ps += [min_p] * sample_lens
                presence_penalties += [p] * sample_lens
                frequency_penalties += [f] * sample_lens
                repetition_penalties += [r] * sample_lens

        if do_penalties:
            for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
                seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
                sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
                if (seq_group.is_prompt
                        and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None):
                    prefill_len = len(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
                    prompt_tokens.extend(
                        array(VLLM_TOKEN_ID_ARRAY_TYPE)
                        for _ in range(prefill_len))
                    output_tokens.extend(
                        array(VLLM_TOKEN_ID_ARRAY_TYPE)
                        for _ in range(prefill_len))
                if seq_group.do_sample:
                    for seq_id in seq_ids:
                        seq_data = seq_group.seq_data[seq_id]
                        prompt_tokens.append(seq_data.prompt_token_ids_array)
                        output_tokens.append(seq_data.output_token_ids_array)

        sampling_tensors = SamplingTensors.from_lists(
            temperatures,
            top_ps,
            top_ks,
            min_ps,
            presence_penalties,
            frequency_penalties,
            repetition_penalties,
            prompt_tokens,
            output_tokens,
            vocab_size,
            device,
            dtype,
        )
        return (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k, do_min_p)

    @classmethod
    def from_lists(
        cls,
        temperatures: list[float],
        top_ps: list[float],
        top_ks: list[int],
        min_ps: list[float],
        presence_penalties: list[float],
        frequency_penalties: list[float],
        repetition_penalties: list[float],
        prompt_tokens: list[array],
        output_tokens: list[array],
        vocab_size: int,
        device: torch.device,
        dtype: torch.dtype,
    ) -> "SamplingTensors":
        # Note that the performance will be very bad without
        # pinned memory.
        pin_memory = is_pin_memory_available()

        do_penalties = prompt_tokens or output_tokens

        if do_penalties:
            prompt_t = make_tensor_with_pad(
                prompt_tokens,
                vocab_size,
                device="cpu",
                dtype=torch.int64,
                pin_memory=pin_memory,
            )
            output_t = make_tensor_with_pad(
                output_tokens,
                vocab_size,
                device="cpu",
                dtype=torch.int64,
                pin_memory=pin_memory,
            )
        else:
            empty_tensor = torch.empty(0, device=device, dtype=torch.long)
            prompt_t = empty_tensor
            output_t = empty_tensor

        temperatures_t = torch.tensor(
            temperatures,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=dtype,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        top_ps_t = torch.tensor(
            top_ps,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=dtype,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        min_ps_t = torch.tensor(
            min_ps,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=dtype,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        presence_penalties_t = torch.tensor(
            presence_penalties,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=dtype,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        frequency_penalties_t = torch.tensor(
            frequency_penalties,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=dtype,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        repetition_penalties_t = torch.tensor(
            repetition_penalties,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=dtype,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        top_ks_t = torch.tensor(
            top_ks,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=torch.int,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        # Because the memory is pinned, we can do non-blocking
        # transfer to device.

        return cls(
            temperatures=temperatures_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
            top_ps=top_ps_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
            top_ks=top_ks_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
            min_ps=min_ps_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
            presence_penalties=presence_penalties_t.to(device=device,
                                                       non_blocking=True),
            frequency_penalties=frequency_penalties_t.to(device=device,
                                                         non_blocking=True),
            repetition_penalties=repetition_penalties_t.to(device=device,
                                                           non_blocking=True),
            prompt_tokens=prompt_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
            output_tokens=output_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
        )

437	    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs

    @property
    def _should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace(self) -> bool:
        """Whether or not the sampler should modify the probability distribution
        of greedily-sampled tokens such that multinomial sampling would sample
        the greedily-sampled token.

        In other words, if True then we set the probability of the greedily-
        sampled token to 1.

        This is used by speculative decoding, which requires that the sampling
        method be encoded into the probability distribution.
        """
        return self.should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace


def _apply_min_tokens_penalty(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """Apply min_tokens penalty which sets stop tokens to -inf if min_tokens
        have not been generated yet
    """
    # list of indices in logits that will be set to -inf
    logits_to_penalize: list[tuple[int, int]] = []
    logits_applied = 0
    for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
        seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
        sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params

        sample_indices = seq_group.sample_indices
        logits_applied += len(sample_indices) + len(
            seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
        if not seq_group.do_sample:
            continue

        start_idx = sample_indices[0]
        min_tokens = sampling_params.min_tokens
        token_ids_to_penalize = sampling_params.all_stop_token_ids
        if min_tokens > 0 and token_ids_to_penalize:
            seqs_to_penalize: list[int] = []
            for j, seq_id in enumerate(seq_ids):
                seq_data = seq_group.seq_data[seq_id]
                if len(seq_data.output_token_ids_array) < min_tokens:
                    seqs_to_penalize.append(j)

            if seqs_to_penalize:
                # convert to the index into logits
                seqs_to_penalize = [start_idx + j for j in seqs_to_penalize]
                # itertools.product pairs each seq index with every token id
                logits_to_penalize.extend(
                    itertools.product(seqs_to_penalize, token_ids_to_penalize))

    if logits_to_penalize:
        # use zip and * to group indices along each dimension
        # eg. [ (1,2), (1,3), (5,6) ] -> ( (1,1,5), (2,3,6) )
        logits[tuple(zip(*logits_to_penalize))] = -float("inf")

    # verifies that no rows in logits were missed unexpectedly
    assert logits_applied == logits.shape[0]
    return logits


def _apply_top_k_top_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    p: torch.Tensor,
    k: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)

    # Apply top-k.
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
    # Get all the top_k values.
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))

    # Apply top-p.
    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
    # at least one
    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))

    # Re-sort the probabilities.
    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1,
                                                    index=logits_idx,
                                                    src=logits_sort)
    return logits


def _apply_min_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    min_p: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    Adapted from
    https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui/blob/3146124ec01f02c8fb1650a6517cf1b60b537aaf/modules/sampler_hijack.py#L16C17-L16C17
    """
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))

    return logits


def _greedy_sample(
    selected_seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample],
    samples: torch.Tensor,
) -> SampleResultType:
    """Run greedy sampling on a given samples.

1	# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
